major.description: The biggest global events — crashes, wars, banking panics, emergency interventions. Always grounded in curated historical sources.

global_crash.description: Broad market crashes affecting multiple asset classes simultaneously (e.g. 2008 GFC, 2020 COVID crash).

banking_panic.description: Systemic banking failures or liquidity crises threatening the financial system (e.g. Lehman collapse, SVB failure).

war_escalation.description: Armed conflicts or geopolitical confrontations that disrupt global markets (e.g. Gulf War oil shock, Russia-Ukraine invasion).

recession_onset.description: Official or widely-recognised start of an economic recession (e.g. NBER-declared contractions).

emergency_policy_intervention.description: Extraordinary central-bank or government actions outside normal policy cadence (e.g. emergency rate cuts, QE launches, bailouts).

major_commodity_shock.description: Extreme commodity price moves driven by supply disruptions or geopolitical events (e.g. 1973 oil embargo, 2022 energy crisis).

impactful.description: Important but secondary events — rate hikes, inflation surprises, oil spikes, policy tone shifts. Grounded in historical data where possible.

hawkish_rate_hike.description: Central bank raises interest rates, tightening monetary policy.

dovish_rate_cut.description: Central bank lowers interest rates, easing monetary policy.

inflation_surprise.description: CPI or PCE print significantly above or below consensus expectations.

policy_guidance_shift.description: Change in forward guidance tone without an immediate rate move (e.g. 'patient' → 'data-dependent').

oil_price_spike.description: Crude oil price jumps >10% in a short period due to supply/demand imbalance.

bond_yield_jump.description: Sharp rise in benchmark government bond yields signalling tightening financial conditions.

safe_haven_rotation.description: Capital flows from risk assets into safe havens (gold, treasuries, CHF/JPY) during uncertainty.

ordinary.description: Small routine market news — sector rotation, earnings tone, macro chatter. Template-based; no historical grounding required.

daily_macro_watch.description: Routine macro data releases and their market reaction (e.g. jobs report in line, PMI steady).

sector_rotation.description: Money moving between sectors (e.g. tech to defensives) without a major catalyst.

earnings_tone.description: General corporate earnings sentiment (e.g. 'mixed earnings season', 'strong bank results').

market_chatter.description: Generic market colour and analyst commentary filling quiet news days.
